questions.stem: This schematic organizes the adrenergic receptor family into its major branches and subtypes. To which single class of membrane receptors do ALL of the receptors depicted here belong?

explanation.summary: Every adrenergic receptor shown in this classification tree (the alpha-1, alpha-2, and beta-1/2/3 subtypes) is a G-protein coupled receptor (GPCR). These seven-transmembrane receptors act through heterotrimeric G proteins to drive downstream second-messenger signaling rather than opening ion channels directly or possessing intrinsic kinase activity.
explanation.whyCorrect: Every adrenergic receptor shown in this classification tree (the alpha-1, alpha-2, and beta-1/2/3 subtypes) is a G-protein coupled receptor (GPCR). These seven-transmembrane receptors act through heterotrimeric G proteins to drive downstream second-messenger signaling rather than opening ion channels directly or possessing intrinsic kinase activity.
